user_message: How should I coach Tom Rodriguez on Lucerne Publishing? This is a renewal account with declining consumption.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 08:22 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

Our team encountered some challenges with consumption trend. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-14 11:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: consumption trend Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-11 13:13 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Lucerne Publishing consumption trend

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-01-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:01:30] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into consumption trend.

[00:05:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:13:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:27:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
